title: athyrium × subchristensenianum
intro: Athyrium × subchristensenianum, also known as the Christmas fern, is a hybrid plant species that is a cross between the Japanese lady fern (Athyrium yokohamaense) and the lady fern (Athyrium filix-femina). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The Christmas fern has been used to treat various health issues, including arthritis, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. The herbal preparations of the Christmas fern can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
